basic_details.job_title: C-DAC Recruitment 2026 – 900+ Contractual Vacancies Across 11 Centres | JIT June 2026 Cycle (Advt. No. CORP/JIT/02/2026)
basic_details.organization_name: Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC), under MeitY, Govt. of India
basic_details.short_information: C-DAC has published separate notifications dated 01 June 2026 for each of its 11 centres. Each centre-specific advertisement covers post details, domain areas, qualification, experience, age limit, and selection instructions. The number of positions is provisional and may vary based on actual project requirements.

important_dates.interview_date: Communicated later by email to shortlisted candidates only

application_fee.details: C-DAC charges no application fee for any position under the JIT June 2026 Recruitment Cycle. This applies to all candidates across all categories — General, EWS, OBC, SC, ST, and PwBD — and across all 11 centres. No payment of any kind is required at any stage of the application process.

age_limit.details: The upper age limit for C-DAC JIT June 2026 posts is calculated as on 20 June 2026. Age relaxation is available to SC/ST, OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, and Government employees as per Government of India norms.

vacancy_details.Key Domains: VLSI, HPC, Cyber Security, AI/ML, Quantum Computing, Embedded Systems, IoT, Cryptography, Automotive
AI/ML, HPC, IoT, Cyber Security, Full Stack, Cloud, Quantum, Data Science, UI/UX
e-Governance, Health Informatics, Embedded Systems, Education & Training, Speech & NLP, Data Centre
Software Development, HPC, AI, VLSI, Quantum, ETL, DevOps, Mobile App, GIS, Project Management
AI/ML, Full Stack, Cyber Security, Robotics, Communication, DevOps, Mobile App
Cyber Security, VLSI/Chip Design, Quantum Computing, Full Stack, Blockchain, Data Analytics
Full Stack, Cyber Security, Database Administration, Network Security, Embedded Systems, Data Centre
Robotics, Software Development, AI, Quantum/HPC, Additive Manufacturing, Cyber Security, IoT, Embedded
Embedded, VLSI, AI/ML, Cyber Security, Full Stack, Software Development

vacancy_details.Qualification: BE/B.Tech OR ME/M.Tech OR PG in relevant domain (50%–60% as applicable)
ITI/NCVT Trade Certificate with 6 years; OR Diploma in Engg. with 1 year; OR Graduate in CS/Electronics/IT with 1 year; OR DOEACC 'O' Level with 5 years; OR DOEACC 'A' Level with 4 years
Graduation (min 50%) with 3 years experience OR PG with 50% at graduate level
BE/B.Tech (60%) OR PG in relevant domain (60%) OR ME/M.Tech OR Ph.D.
vacancy_details.Experience: 0–5 years (Fresher or Experienced depending on centre)

vacancy_details.Position: Senior Project Engineer / Module Lead / Project Leader
vacancy_details.Qualification: BE/B.Tech (60%) OR PG (60%) OR ME/M.Tech OR Ph.D.
CA / LLM / CS / CMA / two-year full-time MBA / LLB or equivalent

vacancy_details.Position: Project Manager / Program Manager / Knowledge Partner
vacancy_details.Qualification: BE/B.Tech (60%) OR PG (60%) OR ME/M.Tech OR Ph.D.

selection_process: C-DAC follows a multi-stage selection process for JIT June 2026 positions. All communications regarding selection stages are sent by email only — no hard copy letters or call letters are dispatched at any stage.
Stage 1 – Initial Academic Screening: All submitted applications are assessed on the basis of academic records

and domain relevance. Only candidates meeting minimum eligibility criteria proceed further. C-DAC reserves the right to raise the minimum cut-off if the applicant volume significantly exceeds the number of vacancies.
Stage 2 – Written Test (if applicable): A written or skill-based test may be conducted at the discretion of individual centres
depending on the number of screened-in candidates. Shortlisted candidates will be notified by email. No TA/DA is paid for attending the written test.

Stage 3 – Personal Interview: Candidates who clear the screening and written test stages are called for a personal interview by the respective C-DAC centre. The interview call is sent by email only. Receipt of an interview call letter does not confirm selection. No TA/DA is paid for attending the interview.

physical_eligibility.details: C-DAC JIT June 2026 is a large-scale national recruitment drive and competition is expected to be moderate to high, particularly in popular domains. The absence of any application fee significantly increases applicant volume across all centres. | Roles in AI/ML, Full Stack Development, Cyber Security, and Software Development at the Project Engineer and Senior Project Engineer levels will attract the largest applicant pools, especially from tier-2 and tier-3 engineering colleges. In contrast, niche domain posts in Quantum Computing, VLSI/Chip Design, Additive Manufacturing, Robotics, and HPC System Software will see comparatively lower competition but demand highly specialised credentials. | Initial screening is where effective filtering happens — CGPA, subject percentage, and domain match are the primary screening parameters. Candidates applying to Noida (e-Governance, Health Informatics) and Pune (200+ positions, wide domain breadth) should expect steeper competition due to location preference and higher vacancy counts. Documenting relevant project work, publications, or domain-specific internships may help distinguish profiles at the screening stage, even where such work is not formally counted as experience.

how_to_apply: Candidates must apply online only through the official C-DAC careers portal. A valid mobile number and active email ID are mandatory, as OTP-based login is used and all subsequent communications are sent by email.
Visit the official C-DAC careers portal at https://careers.cdac.in/.
Select the relevant centre from the list of active advertisements.
Click 'Apply' against the specific position you wish to apply for.
Log in using your registered mobile number and enter the OTP received.
Fill in all required fields in the online application form accurately.
Upload your passport-size photograph in .jpg format (max 400 KB) and your resume/CV in PDF format (max 500 KB).
Submit the form and note down the unique application number generated upon successful submission.
If applying for multiple positions or multiple centres, repeat the process with a separate application for each.

Documents to be produced at the time of document verification or joining include: educational mark sheets and degree certificates from Class 10 onwards, CGPA-to-percentage conversion certificate (if applicable), experience certificates from all previous and current employers, caste/category certificate (if applicable), disability certificate issued by the District Medical Board (for PwBD candidates), date of birth proof, and an NOC from the current employer where applicable.

faq.question: Q1. What is C-DAC JIT June 2026 Recruitment and when can I apply?
faq.answer: C-DAC’s JIT (Just‑In‑Time) June 2026 cycle (Advt. No. CORP/JIT/02/2026) invites online applications for contractual technical and support positions across multiple centres. Online applications open on 01 June 2026 and close on 20 June 2026 (time varies by centre).

faq.question: Q2. Which centres and domains are covered under this recruitment?
faq.answer: Recruitment covers 11 centres: Bengaluru, Chennai, Noida, Pune, Mumbai, Hyderabad, Delhi, Mohali, Kolkata, Thiruvananthapuram, and Guwahati/CINE (North East). Domains include AI/ML, Cyber Security, VLSI, HPC, Embedded Systems, Full‑Stack, Quantum Computing, IoT, Robotics, Data Science and more.

faq.question: Q3. What posts are available and what are the typical salary ranges?
faq.answer: Positions include Project Associate, Project Technician, Project Engineer, Senior Project Engineer / Module Lead, Project Manager, Project Officer, Administrative Associate, and others. Consolidated salaries typically range from Rs. 20,200–Rs. 1,60,000 per month depending on role and centre (e.g., Project Associate: Rs. 28,750–35,000; Senior Project Engineer: Rs. 60,000–75,000; Project Manager up to Rs. 1,60,000).

faq.question: Q4. What are the educational and experience eligibility criteria?
faq.answer: Eligibility varies by position: BE/B.Tech, ME/M.Tech, MCA, Ph.D., or relevant graduation/post‑graduation in ICT&E domains are common. Experience bands differ by post (fresher to 15+ years). Qualifications must be from AICTE/UGC‑approved or recognized institutions. Final semester candidates may apply but must produce final marks to join.

faq.question: Q5. Is there an application fee?
faq.answer: No. C-DAC charges no application fee for any post or category under this JIT June 2026 cycle.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process?
faq.answer: Multi‑stage: initial screening (based on academic records and experience), written/skill test (if applicable), and personal interview. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted by email. No TA/DA is provided for tests or interviews.

faq.question: Q7. Can I apply to multiple centres or multiple posts?
faq.answer: Yes, but you must submit separate online applications for each post/centre you wish to be considered for. Each post has a separate advertisement and vacancy list.

faq.question: Q8. What documents must I upload and keep ready for verification?
faq.answer: Upload at application: passport‑size photo (.jpg) and CV/resume (PDF). For document verification/joining: mark sheets and degree certificates from Class 10 onwards, CGPA‑to‑percentage conversion (if applicable), experience certificates with detailed employer info, category certificate (SC/ST/OBC/EWS), PwBD certificate (if applicable), proof of DOB, and NOC if currently employed and required.

faq.question: Q9. Are these permanent jobs? Will candidates be posted anywhere in India?
faq.answer: No. These are purely contractual engagements (typically up to 3 years or project duration; Kolkata may have 1‑year terms). There is no right to regular/ permanent employment. Selected candidates can be deputed or transferred to any project location across India as per requirement.

faq.question: Q10. Where do I apply and whom should I contact for centre‑specific queries?
faq.answer: Apply online via the C‑DAC careers portal: https://careers.cdac.in/ — select the relevant centre advertisement and apply. Centre‑specific recruitment emails are listed in the respective notifications (e.g., [email protected] for Bengaluru, chn‑[email protected] for Chennai, hr‑[email protected] for Noida). All official updates and corrigenda will be on https://www.cdac.in/ and the careers portal.
